news.headline: Verizon waives call/text/data fees for customers in SoCal due to wildfires and windstorm Jan 9-18.
news.summary: From January 9 to January 18, customers in the most severely affected counties of California will not be charged for any calls, texts, or data usage.

news.headline: BoE Breeden: What is key for the BoE is how the rest of the world reacts to Trump.
BoE Breeden: I don't have a particular path for the bank rate in my mind right now.
Freddie Mac: 30-year fixed-rate mortgage averaged 6.93% as of january 9th.
Bureau Veritas reports share count and voting rights as of December 31, 2024.
Insignia Capital Group in Walnut Creek, CA welcomes Tiffany Obenchain back as Partner after her tenure from 2014.
news.summary: Today, Insignia Capital Group, a private equity firm located in Walnut Creek, California, shared that Tiffany Obenchain has returned to the company as a Partner. Tiffany had previously worked with Insignia as an investment professional from 2014 to present.

news.headline: Dassault Aviation: Reports aircraft deliveries, orders, and backlog figures as of December 31, 2024.
Microsoft and Google contribute $1M to Trump's inauguration fund.
Park City Strike resolved after nearly two weeks, resulting in higher wages for ski patrollers. Holiday period affected, sparking online debate.
news.summary: The strike lasting almost two weeks severely impacted the biggest ski resort in the U.S. during a peak holiday season. It also led to widespread online criticism regarding the increasing economic disparity in rural mountain regions.

news.headline: OnPay secures $100M+ funding with $63M Series B led by Carrick Capital Partners, AB Private Credit Investors, and MC Credit Partners.
news.summary: OnPay, a top provider of payroll, HR, and benefits solutions, revealed that it has successfully acquired over $100 million in funding. The funding includes a $63 million Series B funding round spearheaded by Carrick Capital Partners, with contributions from AB Private Credit Investors and current investors, alongside a debt facility offered by MC Credit Partners. This significant funding marks a pivotal moment for the company.

news.headline: Wolters Kluwer named Leader in EHS software by Verdantix Green Quadrant: EHS Software 2025 report.
news.summary: Wolters Kluwer, a renowned provider of professional information, software solutions, and services worldwide, has recently been recognized as a Leader in the Verdantix Green Quadrant: EHS Software 2025 analysis. This report offers an in-depth, data-driven evaluation of the top 21 environment, health, and safety (EHS) platform solutions available. Verdantix, a respected research and advisory firm known for driving innovative advancements globally, conducted the study. The acknowledgment underscores Wolters Kluwer's position as a prominent player in the industry, reflecting its commitment to excellence in the EHS sector.

news.headline: Lemonway launches Online Onboarding solution for marketplaces, streamlining merchant process. Helps marketplaces focus on growth.
news.summary: A European payment institution, Lemonway, has recently launched Online Onboarding, a new solution aimed at improving the merchant onboarding process for marketplaces. This innovation aims to simplify and enhance the onboarding process for marketplaces by eliminating the need for them to handle new customer onboarding internally. This allows marketplaces to prioritize strategic initiatives and business expansion while maintaining competitiveness with their partners. The turnkey solution is now available, offering a seamless and efficient onboarding experience for merchants.

news.headline: Palm Beach Capital invests in MBI Companies, a Knoxville architecture firm; launching national design services platform.
news.summary: A private equity fund based in West Palm Beach, Florida, Palm Beach Capital Fund VI, L.P. ("PBC"), has revealed the acquisition of a stake in MBI Companies, Inc. ("MBI Companies"), an established architecture and engineering design firm in Knoxville, Tennessee. This move is part of PBC's initiative to establish a national architecture and design services firm. The investment has been made through one of PBC's entities, signaling the launch of a new investment platform. This investment marks a strategic step towards the goal of building a prominent presence in the architecture and design sector.

news.headline: DXC teams up with Ferrari to enhance infotainment systems for upcoming vehicles, including F80 supercar.
news.summary: ASHBURN, Va. - On January 9, 2025, DXC (listed as NYSE: DXC) revealed a new deal with Ferrari to enhance their collaboration in crafting advanced in-vehicle infotainment systems for Ferrari's latest supercar, the F80. This agreement marks a significant development in their partnership and aims to pave the way for future innovations and upgrades in Ferrari's infotainment technology.

news.headline: Aecon Group Inc. plans to release Q4 & year-end 2024 financial results on March 5, 2025, with a live call on March 6 at 9am ET.
news.summary: Aecon Group Inc. (TSX: ARE) disclosed plans to unveil its financial results for the fourth quarter and year-end of 2024 on March 5, 2025, after the markets close. The release will be followed by a live webcast and conference call scheduled for March 6, 2025, at 9 a.m. (Eastern Time). The company aims to provide detailed insights into its performance during this period, offering investors and stakeholders an opportunity to understand its financial standing as of the mentioned time frames. The event will be a pivotal moment for those interested in the company's progress, with expectations running high as to what the numbers will reveal.

news.headline: Circle donates $1M USDC to Trump's Inauguration Committee. Stablecoin issuers like Tether and Circle are top US debt buyers.
news.summary: Stablecoin issuers such as Tether and Circle, who are overcollateralized, have become significant players in the US government debt market. Together, they rank as the 18th largest buyers worldwide in this market.

news.headline: Sonde Health integrates voice-based health monitoring technology into Qualcomm's Snapdragon S7+ Sound Platform, allowing tracking via audio devices.
news.summary: In Boston, a recent development in cognitive health has emerged as Sonde Health has successfully incorporated voice-based monitoring technology into Qualcomm’s Snapdragon S7+ Sound Platform. This integration allows for health tracking to be conducted through everyday audio devices. This advancement signifies a significant step forward in leveraging technology for health monitoring purposes, marking the importance of integrating cognitive health tracking into widely used platforms.

news.headline: Alex Shikany promoted to Executive Vice President of A3, overseeing key operations. He brings over a decade of experience.
news.summary: Ann Arbor, Michigan - The Association for Advancing Automation (A3), a prominent automation trade association in North America, has recently promoted Alex Shikany to the position of executive vice president. With over ten years of experience and expertise in leadership and innovation, Alex previously held the role of vice president of Membership & Market Intelligence. In his new capacity, he will be responsible for managing crucial operations for A3 and serving as the primary point of contact for the A3 Board.

news.headline: Net at Work acquires e2b teknologies' Sage and Acumatica practices, enhancing ERP services. - 38 characters
news.summary: Leading technology provider Net at Work has enhanced its ERP and business applications services through a recent acquisition. The company, known as North America's largest advisor for small and mid-size businesses, finalized the integration of e2b into its operations. This move signifies Net at Work's commitment to expanding its service offerings and capabilities. The acquisition is poised to significantly impact the company's portfolio, with plans to leverage e2b's expertise to better serve their clientele.

news.headline: Brown and Caldwell got $650,000 from The Water Research Foundation for climate projects. Collaboration aims to address water challenges.
news.summary: Joint research efforts are being undertaken to address challenges in the water industry. Based in Walnut Creek, California, engineering consulting company Brown and Caldwell is at the forefront, offering innovative water and environmental solutions across North America and the Pacific region. The collaboration aims to address pressing issues in the industry and implement effective strategies. This initiative comes as part of a broader effort to enhance sustainability and efficiency. The company's extensive experience and expertise position them well to lead this initiative, with a focus on delivering tangible outcomes.

news.headline: Viridien meets financial goals for 2024 with net cash flow of c. $50M, exceeding target of $30M, and bond repurchase of $60M, improving credit rating.
news.summary: On January 9, 2025, at 5:45 PM CET in Paris, Viridien's revenue and EBITDA were reported to be in line with expectations. The net cash flow for 2024 reached approximately $50 million, surpassing earlier projections. Sophie Zurquiyah, the CEO, expressed satisfaction with the company's performance, noting that they exceeded their $30 million target with EBITDA growth and strong net cash flow generation. Viridien also repurchased $60 million worth of their bonds, double the initial commitment of $30 million. Additionally, they saw an improvement in their credit rating from S&P and extended their revolving credit facility for debt refinancing efforts. The success was attributed to the Data, Digital & Energy Transition segment, particularly with robust growth in Geoscience activities and the launch of the notable Laconia project. This project aimed to enhance the value of Earth Data's seismic data library. Further details can be found on benzinga.com.

news.headline: OPKO Health (OPK) to present at J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference on Jan 15, 2025, in San Francisco at 3:00 p.m. PT.
news.summary: On January 9, 2025, OPKO Health, Inc. revealed their involvement in the 43rd Annual J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference scheduled from January 13-16, 2025, at Westin St. Francis hotel in San Francisco. The company, listed as Nasdaq: OPK, will engage in individual investor meetings while also conducting a presentation on Wednesday, January 15th at 3:00 p.m. Pacific time during the event.

news.headline: Global Jet Capital raises $617M through securitization, increasing total assets to $5.8B and bonds to $4.8B.
news.summary: In Danbury, CT on September 17, 2024, Global Jet Capital disclosed the completion of its BJETS 2024-2 securitization, garnering around $617 million. This marks the eighth ABS offering by Global Jet Capital, elevating the total securitized assets to roughly $5.8 billion and the bonds issued to approximately $4.8 billion.

news.headline: Hepsiburada announces second issuance of asset-backed securities, totaling TRY 350 million on Sept. 30, 2024.
news.summary: On September 30, 2024, a prominent Turkish e-commerce platform known as D-MARKET Electronic Services & Trading, operating under the name "Hepsiburada," revealed the launch of their latest asset-backed securities. The issuance amounts to a substantial total of TRY 350 million. This move signifies a significant financial development for the company as they continue to strengthen their position within the e-commerce sector.

news.headline: Azerion seeks EUR 50M in new bonds, Nasdaq Stockholm trading, avoids US distribution due to securities regulations.
news.summary: This information is not intended for distribution to any individual located or residing in the United States of America or to any U.S. person, as defined in Regulation S under the U.S. Securities Act of 1933, as amended, or in any jurisdiction where distribution of this document is unlawful.

news.headline: NowCM and White & Case have set up the first fully digital, end-to-end automated commercial paper programme for Haniel.
Nike's Jordan Brand Design Chief, Jason Mayden, discusses brand creation with a16z crypto. Learn more about his insights.
news.summary: In an engaging discussion with a16z crypto, Jason Mayden, the Chief Design Officer at Nike's Jordan Brand, delves into the strategic nuances of brand development across various sectors. Mayden, a key figure at one of the leading sports brands, shares insights on leveraging design and innovation to create a powerful brand identity. The conversation sheds light on the critical role of brand building in today's competitive market landscape. This exchange serves as a valuable resource for industry professionals looking to enhance their branding strategies.

news.headline: Guggenheim Q4 2024 outlook: Fed rate cuts are positive for leveraged credit, but impact on high yield bonds may vary.
news.summary: The impact of interest rate reductions on high yield bonds could result in a combination of positive and negative effects. While a decrease in rates may lead to lower borrowing costs for companies issuing these bonds, it can also potentially reduce the overall yields for investors. This dynamic could result in mixed outcomes for individuals holding or considering investments in high yield bonds. It is essential for investors to carefully monitor how rate cuts, particularly significant ones, may influence the performance and returns of high yield bonds in the current economic environment.

news.headline: In 2024, NVIDIA AI fuels robotics in ocean cleanup, surgeries, farming, and more, reshaping industries. Read on for details.
news.summary: Discover the cutting-edge robotics technology driven by NVIDIA AI, which encompasses various applications such as autonomous ocean cleaning, humanoid robots, surgical assistance, and agricultural innovations poised to revolutionize sectors by the year 2024. These developments signify a significant leap in technological progress, showcasing the remarkable potential of AI-powered robotics across diverse fields.

news.headline: Silicon Valley giants revive Republican bank regulatory priority for Trump's second administration.
news.summary: A recent lobbying effort by prominent tech leaders in Silicon Valley is revitalizing a key Republican initiative on bank regulations, set to make a significant resurgence in the upcoming term of the Trump administration.

news.headline: Navigator Gas successfully places USD 100 million of senior unsecured bonds in the Nordic bond market. Bonds mature in 2029 with 7.25% fixed coupon.
news.summary: On October 17, 2024, Navigator Holdings Ltd., also known as Navigator Gas, disclosed that it has effectively issued USD 100 million worth of fresh senior unsecured bonds in the Nordic bond market. These newly issued bonds are set to reach maturity in October 2029 and will offer a fixed coupon rate of 7.25% annually, dispersed semi-annually in arrears. Navigator Gas stands as the proprietor and operator of the globe's most extensive fleet of handysize liquefied gas carriers, traded under the ticker symbol NVGS on the New York Stock Exchange.

news.headline: Bitcoin could reach over $150K in 2025 due to increased institutional, corporate, and nation-state adoption, predicts Galaxy Research.
news.summary: A research conducted by Galaxy Research projects a substantial increase for Bitcoin in the year 2025, propelled by adoption from institutions, corporations, and nation-states. The research suggests that the value of BTC could potentially surge to $150,000.

news.headline: Trusted by Wall Street in Atkins.
news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has reached an all-time low of 3.8%, with over 250,000 new jobs created in the last quarter. The report also shows that the GDP has increased by 5.2% in the same period, marking a significant growth in the economy. Additionally, there has been a 12% rise in foreign investment, totaling $500 million in the last year. Experts attribute this positive trend to government policies aimed at boosting employment and economic growth.

news.headline: Mortgage market in Hong Kong shows growth in Nov 2024 with 9.7% rise in applications and loans approved at HK$24.2 billion.
news.summary: In November 2024, Hong Kong Monetary Authority revealed a 9.7% surge in mortgage applications. The loans approved during the same period amounted to HK$24.2 billion.

news.headline: Trump opts for traditional nominees for economic team, avoiding controversial choices. Embraced  by both parties.
news.summary: President-elect Donald Trump has opted for experienced individuals for his economic team, selecting Wall Street veterans and established Republican figures for key roles in his administration. His choices reflect a traditional approach, with no out-of-the-box picks.

news.headline: PIMCO Canada plans to merge three funds (PTI, PTO, PIX) into PMEI, managed by PIMCO Canada.
news.summary: In Toronto on October 18, 2024, PIMCO Canada Corp. shared additional information regarding the planned mergers of three funds. The proposed mergers involve PIMCO Tactical Income Fund (TSX: PTI), PIMCO Tactical Income Opportunities Fund (TSX: PTO), and PIMCO Multi-Sector Income Fund (TSX: PIX), which will combine to form a new closed-end fund managed by PIMCO Canada called PIMCO Monthly Enhanced Income Fund ("PMEI"). This consolidation aims to streamline operations and enhance fund management.

news.headline: GOP wants to integrate two priorities in upcoming tax legislation regarding Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.
news.summary: There is discussion among Republican lawmakers about combining two key objectives into a single agreement within the upcoming tax legislation required next year.

news.headline: Mercer Intl Inc. announces $200M private offering of 12.875% senior notes due Oct. 1, 2028.
news.summary: On October 23, 2024, Mercer International Inc. disclosed the pricing details of its private placement of Additional Notes. The company, listed under Nasdaq as MERC, is offering $200.0 million in total principal value for the 12.875% senior notes, maturing on October 1, 2028.

news.headline: Honda and Nissan are contemplating merging to adapt in the evolving auto market. Yet, many carmaker mergers struggle or collapse.
news.summary: Japanese companies are exploring the possibility of partnering together to navigate the evolving auto industry landscape. This move comes as the industry faces significant upheaval and transformation. It is no secret that the history of the auto industry is rife with unsuccessful and troubled collaborations. The companies are looking to combine their strengths and resources in the hopes of achieving success in this challenging environment.

news.headline: NY Fed's consumer survey: More Americans expect financial situation to improve next year.
news.summary: According to the latest monthly consumer survey conducted by the New York Fed, there has been a notable increase in the percentage of Americans who anticipate an enhancement in their financial situation in the upcoming year.

news.headline: A.I. adoption to boost midsize cities in Midwest, Mid-Atlantic, South - new research suggests.
news.summary: New research forecasts that several midsize cities in the Midwest, Mid-Atlantic, and South regions, which were formerly struggling, could see positive impacts as technology becomes more prevalent.

news.headline: HK Monetary Authority promotes digital lai-see for CNY 2025 to boost sustainability, with note exchange services. #HKMA #CNY2025
news.summary: To foster sustainability during Chinese New Year 2025, the Hong Kong Monetary Authority is promoting the use of electronic lai-see. They have introduced new services for exchanging notes, aiming to reduce paper wastage. This initiative underscores the growing shift towards digital transactions and highlights the importance of embracing innovative methods for traditional practices. By leveraging technology, the authority hopes to minimize environmental impact and enhance efficiency. This move is expected to resonate with a wide audience, as it reflects the evolving nature of celebrations and financial transactions. The initiative aligns with the authority's commitment to advancing sustainable practices, with a focus on encouraging eco-friendly alternatives. Embracing electronic lai-see not only adapts to modern trends but also supports the broader goal of reducing paper usage. This push towards digitalization showcases a forward-thinking approach to celebrating cultural events while aligning with broader environmental objectives.

news.headline: Yellen provides aid to Ukraine while Trump's re-election sparks optimism for lower taxes and regulations.
news.summary: The re-election of Trump has increased confidence among business executives that there will be a decrease in taxes and regulations in the near future.

news.headline: PIMCO closed-end funds announce monthly common share distributions payable on December 2, 2024, to shareholders of record on Nov 12, 2024.
news.summary: On November 1, 2024, the Boards of Trustees/Directors of PIMCO's closed-end funds authorized monthly distributions for the common shares of each Fund. The distributions will be paid out on December 2, 2024, to shareholders recorded on November 12, 2024. The ex-dividend date is also on November 12, 2024.

news.headline: Big Bank CEOs, including BofA's Moynihan, optimistic about Trump's regulatory changes for aggressive move as rates decline.
news.summary: Brian Moynihan, CEO of Bank of America, expressed optimism regarding regulatory changes, stating they will allow companies to be more assertive as interest rates decrease.

news.headline: In 2024, Render Network hits milestones with token upgrades, 3D tool support, AI integrations, and community growth, preparing for 2025.
news.summary: In 2024, Render Network accomplished several milestones by implementing upgrades to its token, expanding support for 3D tools, integrating artificial intelligence, and experiencing substantial growth in its community. These achievements pave the way for the year 2025, indicating a promising future for the network.

news.headline: California economy suffers from Hollywood studio struggles as film production stagnates post-strikes, facing tougher competition elsewhere.
news.summary: Following significant strikes last year, the film production industry has struggled to recover, facing increased competition from other areas. This has resulted in a decrease in the number of productions and challenges for the sector. The future of the industry remains uncertain as it grapples with these hurdles.

news.headline: Chinese corporations avoid Trump’s tariffs by finding new paths to US, showcasing limits of promised tariffs.
news.summary: Businesses have identified numerous fresh pathways to access the American market, showcasing the constraints of the tariffs pledged by Donald Trump. This highlights the adaptability and resilience of companies in navigating challenges, with the potential implications on trade and economic relations between the involved parties.

news.headline: Empire Communities closes CAD$225 million senior notes offering, replaces 7.375% notes due Dec. 15, 2025 with 7.625% notes due Nov. 1, 2029.
news.summary: Vaughan, Ontario - On November 4, 2024, Empire Communities Corp. revealed the completion of a private placement offering for CAD$225 million in 7.625% senior unsecured notes due on November 1, 2029. These notes were sold at par and are guaranteed by specified subsidiaries of Empire. Interest on the notes will be paid semi-annually on May 1 and November 1 each year, with the first payment scheduled for May 1, 2025.

news.headline: Debate on U.S. sanctions for Russia over Ukraine war intensifies as president-elect pledges to use them sparingly.
news.summary: The incoming president has announced plans to minimize the use of sanctions and focus on resolving the conflict in Ukraine. This has raised concerns about the effectiveness of sanctions and sparked debates on their utility.
